text:
We introduce a new speech recognition system called Deep Speech, based entirely on deep learning techniques. Deep Speech is different than other speech recognition systems in that it has a greatly simplified architecture. While other systems have many detailed tweaks and processing steps that must be manually defined, with Deep Speech these steps are instead learned by the algorithm. This results in a system that is resilient to noise, echo and other aspects of real speech even without the need for a phoneme recognition dictionary and explicit noise reduction pre-processing, all of which would be required in a traditional system.

We describe a system for speech recognition that uses a highly optimized recurrent neural network (RNN) training engine that makes efficient use of multiple GPUs, as well as new data synthesis methods that enable rapid generation of large quantities of training data with a wide range of characteristics. Our system, Deep Speech, achieves a new record for Switchboard Hub5’00, the most widely used standard for conversational telephone speech recognition with 16.0% error rate on the full test set. It also surpasses the performance of today’s leading commercial speech recognition systems on difficult, noisy audio streams by replacing a large amount of human ingenuity with deep learning.
headline: Fully Human Written
